title: calamagrostis neglecta var. hyperborea
intro: Calamagrostis neglecta var. hyperborea, also known as Northern Fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Calamagrostis neglecta var. hyperborea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is typically used to treat minor health issues such as sore throats and digestive problems.
